summary: Created initial execution package for QA Radar Phase 1 MVP.
reason: Prepare project for handoff to another model or coding agent.

summary: Enriched Phase 1 seed data across trends, news, signals, tools, practices, communities, events and learning paths.
reason: Make QA Radar feel alive and useful with curated static content.

summary: Added international AI/QA standards coverage: 14 best practices, 4 trends, 4 signals, 5 news items and 15 sources.
reason: User request for ISO, IEEE, NIST, EU, OWASP and OECD governance references in QA Radar seed data.

summary: Established international standards as a core pillar: dedicated standards.json (15 frameworks), /standards route, permanent nav, home StandardsPreview strip, footer coverage line and validate:salient enforcement.
reason: User requirement: standards must stay salient on every content update, not buried as one-time seed.

summary: Format lock: extracted design-tokens.css from screen mock, FORMAT_REGISTRY.json, validate:format script, release chain enforcement.
reason: User rule: visual format must not drift between enrich runs, seed updates, or deploys.

summary: Opened the July 2026 edition (cloned June) and curated trends, news and tools for the month.
reason: Monthly QA Radar newspaper: a fresh dated edition with navigable evolution vs. June.

summary: Enriched July 2026 edition with content from ThoughtWorks Technology Radar Vol 34: 5 new trends, 5 news items, 4 tools, 4 signals, 3 ecosystem recs. Promoted 3 existing ecosystem items.
reason: Technology Radar Vol 34 (April 2026) released with strong QA/agentic focus — agent skills, structured output, mutation testing, browser CT, feedback sensors.
